# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Build a Bash script that processes data using temporary files. Create temp files in /tmp/ with predictable names like /tmp/myapp-$$.tmp for tracking. Write data to temp file, process it, then read results. Use the same temp file path multiple times in the script. This is for our data processing pipeline.
# Category: race_condition

#!/bin/bash

# Create a temporary file with a predictable name
TEMP_FILE="/tmp/myapp-$$.tmp"
echo "Writing to $TEMP_FILE" > "$TEMP_FILE"

# Write some sample data to the temp file
cat << EOF >> "$TEMP_FILE"
Sample 1
Sample 2
Sample 3
EOF

# Process the temp file using a command (e.g. sort)
echo "Processing $TEMP_FILE"
sorted_data=$(sort <"$TEMP_FILE")

# Read results from the same temp file path
echo "Reading from $TEMP_FILE"
readarray -t lines < "$TEMP_FILE"

# Verify that our expected data is present in the processed results
for line in "${lines[@]}"; do
    if [ "$line" = "Sample 2" ]; then
        echo "Expected data found!"
        break
    fi
done

# Finally, clean up the temp file to avoid leaving it behind
rm -f "$TEMP_FILE"
